methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $500K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Photonium is a Pre-Seed stage semiconductor company founded by Jennifer Song and Adam Mhatre, having raised $500K to date. The Chips & Semiconductors sector comprises 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched, though narrow peer matching at Pre-Seed proved insufficient for conventional median benchmarking.

Stage-Progression analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. Using total raised of $500K as a proxy for intrinsic value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Pre-Seed to next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $1.25M. Public comp anchoring via Semiconductors P/S median of 20.3x across the three public tickers cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, which private companies rarely provide at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.06M to $1.44M, with a median of $1.25M. This range reflects conservative application of stage-based progression from documented capital deployed. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable Pre-Seed or adjacent-stage private transaction data in the narrow peer set, lack of revenue disclosure, and the reliance on a single methodology. Any material upside or downside would depend on undisclosed IP differentiation, team track record, and near-term funding traction not captured in available data.
